blocks.text: Dear Kelly Klein:

Thank you for the confidence you and your colleagues at Vance and Sons have placed in our firm. This letter confirms the terms on which Gladepoint Strategies LLC will conduct a market entry assessment for {{fact:f:E-2022-001.client}}. It becomes effective when signed by both of us below.

The assessment will answer three questions: which adjacent markets deserve your attention, what it would take to compete in each, and how entry should be sequenced. We will ground the answers in interviews with your commercial team, conversations with customers and distributors, and our own analysis of market size and competitive position. You will receive a written assessment, a recommended entry sequence, and the supporting analysis in full, presented to your executive team at the close of the work.

Caroline Mayo, a Principal of the firm, will lead the engagement and be your day-to-day counterpart. She will be supported by Jeff Pope, Engagement Manager, who will run the workplan and keep your office informed, and John Johnson, Senior Analyst, who will build the market and financial analysis. I will stay close to the work and will read every deliverable before it reaches you.

We intend to keep the effort light on your organization. Jeff will agree a single point of contact and a standing weekly call with your office, and we will ask for data once, in writing, rather than piecemeal. Our fee for the engagement is a fixed {{fact:f:E-2022-001.fee}}, inclusive of professional time, with reasonable travel expenses billed at cost. We propose to begin work on {{fact:f:E-2022-001.start}} and expect the assessment to run approximately four months from that date.

We do not take on a new client relationship lightly, and we are glad to be starting this one. If the terms above match your understanding, please sign where indicated and return a copy to my attention. We look forward to the work.
Kickoff Memo: Market Entry Assessment
From: Caroline Mayo. To: John Johnson, Jeff Pope.

We start fieldwork for {{fact:f:E-2022-001.client}} on {{fact:f:E-2022-001.start}}. The job is to tell Vance and Sons which markets to enter, in what order, and what it will cost them to compete. Our sponsor is Kelly Klein, General Manager. Everything we produce should hold up in front of her operating team, so write for them, not for us.
Workstreams and owners:

items: Market sizing and segmentation, John. Bottom-up model, one tab per segment, every assumption footnoted.
Competitive assessment, Caroline with John. Who wins in each segment today and why.
Client interviews and data collection, Jeff. One request list, one point of contact in Kelly's office, no piecemeal asks.
Entry economics and sequencing, all hands in the final month.

blocks.text: Next steps:

items: Jeff circulates the workplan and interview list this week.
John stands up the model shell before fieldwork begins.
First team check-in is Friday morning, thirty minutes, standing.

blocks.text: Market Entry Assessment
Prepared for {{fact:f:E-2022-001.client}} by Gladepoint Strategies LLC. Presented by Caroline Mayo, Principal.
Where We Are

items: Fieldwork underway since {{fact:f:E-2022-001.start}}
Interviews with the Vance and Sons commercial team complete
Market model drafted; segment sizing in review
Distributor conversations scheduled through the month

blocks.text: What We Are Learning

items: Demand is concentrated in fewer segments than expected
Competitors defend on service, not price
Channel access, not product, is the gating question

blocks.text: Team

items: Caroline Mayo, Principal: engagement lead
John Johnson, Senior Analyst: market model and exhibits
Jeff Pope, Engagement Manager: workplan, interviews, reporting
Kelly Klein, General Manager: client sponsor

blocks.text: Next Steps

items: Complete distributor interviews and close the market model
Working session with the sponsor team to pressure-test early findings
Steering update on the standing call

blocks.text: Kelly Klein, John Johnson, Jeff Pope --

Thanks for yesterday's session. Short version: we are on track, and nothing in the early findings changes the shape of the work for {{fact:f:E-2022-001.client}}. The deck is attached; Kelly, feel free to share it with the wider Vance and Sons team.

Two asks. Kelly, we still need the distributor introductions your office is holding; Jeff will send the final list Monday, please confirm the three we flagged. John is waiting on the regional volume file to close out the model, same timing.
Next update stays on the standing call. Shout if anything above needs to move.
Caroline Mayo
Meeting Minutes: Market Entry Assessment Working Session
Prepared by Jeff Pope, Engagement Manager. Working session held by video call with the {{fact:f:E-2022-001.client}} sponsor team.
Attendees:

items: Caroline Mayo, Principal, Gladepoint Strategies
John Johnson, Senior Analyst, Gladepoint Strategies
Jeff Pope, Engagement Manager, Gladepoint Strategies (minutes)
Kelly Klein, General Manager, Vance and Sons

blocks.text: Caroline opened with the emerging picture: demand is concentrated in fewer segments than the team expected, and channel access looks like the deciding question for entry. Kelly agreed the finding matches what her regional managers report and asked that the final recommendation address distributor terms directly.

John walked the group through the market model. The segment sizing held up under questioning; two assumptions on repeat purchase rates still need support from the volume file Kelly's office is pulling together. The group agreed the model is close enough to begin drafting entry scenarios in parallel.
Action items:

rows: Deliver regional volume file to the team
Update repeat purchase assumptions once the file lands
Draft entry scenarios for internal review
Circulate minutes and the revised workplan

blocks.text: Status Report: Market Entry Assessment
Prepared by Caroline Mayo, Principal, Gladepoint Strategies LLC, for the {{fact:f:E-2022-001.client}} executive team.
Status summary

The engagement is in its final phase and on schedule. The market model is closed, entry scenarios are drafted, and we are preparing the written assessment. Work remains within the agreed fixed fee of {{fact:f:E-2022-001.fee}}; we are not requesting any change to scope or budget.
Accomplishments this period

items: Closed the market model, including the repeat purchase assumptions now supported by the regional volume file
Completed the distributor interviews and folded the findings into the channel assessment
Drafted three entry scenarios with sequencing and investment implications, reviewed with your sponsor team
John Johnson completed the segment exhibits; every assumption is footnoted to its source

blocks.text: Upcoming work

items: Complete the written assessment and the recommended entry sequence
Jeff Pope to circulate the review draft to the Vance and Sons team and schedule the closing readout
Final steering presentation over the standing video call

blocks.text: Risks

Two items bear watching. Timing of internal review on the Vance and Sons side is the main schedule risk; we have held a buffer week to absorb it. Separately, one distributor declined to speak with us, and we have noted in the draft where that limits our confidence in a single regional estimate. Neither risk changes the recommendation taking shape.
